title: Black Ash Vase
line: For a branch, or for nothing.
description: A narrow vessel for a single stem or an empty corner — matte stoneware that prefers shadow to spotlight.
narrativeTitle: Emptiness as composition — the wall reads differently with one stem.
narrativeBody: Stoneware here is not loud; it holds light the way paper holds ink — slowly, without argument.
atmosphereQuote: Sometimes the object is there to teach you what not to fill.
